methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $41M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Dragonfly Aerospace, founded by Bryan Dean, is a Series G stage company in Space & Aerospace that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though narrow-filter peer matching proved constrained.

Given data limitations, only the Stage-Progression method was reliably applicable. Using $41M as a total-raised proxy and applying a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical for Series G advancement yields $51M. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable—while 23 same-sector companies exist, only 2 fell within narrow stage and tier filters, below the 3-datapoint threshold for statistical defensibility. Public-Comp Anchoring similarly could not proceed; though the Space & Aerospace sector trades at a 64.2x median P/S across the 5 public tickers, Dragonfly's revenue is not on file, preventing meaningful multiple application.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single method, supports a valuation range of $44M (low) to $59M (high), with a median estimate of $51M. Key uncertainties center on the absence of comparable private valuations at Dragonfly's specific stage and the company's unreported revenue, which would anchor public-market multiple approaches. Additional disclosure of financial metrics or identification of closer-stage peer transactions would materially strengthen this analysis.
